page: Microsoft Azure DC8ds_v3 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, resulting in 8 vCPUs. Memory capacity is 64.0 GB, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Storage is bundled with a 614 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark results place the DC8ds_v3 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1676.0 and a multi-core score of 8918.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 17106.17. Memory bandwidth tests show 843.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 88.28 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 49.21 GB/sec for large-block writes. In database operations, the server processed 16.96 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 7754.48 in Passmark database operations. Notably, the instance demonstrated strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 283.3 tokens/sec for a 135M model, though medium 7B model text generation remains average at 11.93 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC8ds_v3 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 combined with dedicated physical cores and local storage, providing a balanced resource density for workloads that require secure, memory-intensive environments without the overhead of hyperthreading. The presence of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, supports secure computing tasks. This hardware profile is suited for database operations, memory-cached applications, secure data processing, and moderate web serving workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure DC8ds_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, configured with 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a 614 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 17106.17 and a Redis SET throughput of 16.96 million operations per second. However, it exhibits strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 283.3 tokens per second. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated core allocation make this instance suitable for memory-optimized workloads, database operations, and secure data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC8ds_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 614 GB local disk, optimized for secure database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC8ds_v3 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with 64 GB RAM, a 614 GB local disk, and dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, high memory density, and local storage for secure, data-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
